data.title: Rio Tinto donates A$1.5 million to support Qld and NT communities impacted by Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle and recent widespread flooding
data.description: In Melbourne, Australia, Rio Tinto has announced a donation of A$1.5 million to aid disaster relief in Queensland and the Northern Territory after they were hit by Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle and flooding. The donation will support immediate relief efforts and enhance preparedness for future emergencies. A$1 million will go towards assisting Queensland, with the Australian Red Cross and State Emergency Service benefiting from the funds to bolster response and preparedness measures.

data.title: CrowdStrike Accelerates SMB Cybersecurity Transformation Across JAPAC with Expanded Distributor-Led Services
data.description: In Texas and Vietnam, CrowdStrike (NASDAQ: CRWD) has revealed plans to broaden its Managed Security Service Provider (MSSP) approach in Japan and Asia Pacific (JAPAC). This move aims to enhance SMBs' reach to the CrowdStrike Falcon® platform and speed up AI-powered cybersecurity evolution. In collaboration with Dicker Data and Otsuka Corporation, key distributors, CrowdStrike is set to bolster its strategic partnerships as part of this expansion strategy.

data.title: EQT raises Asia Pacific's largest private equity fund, closing BPEA IX at USD 15.6 billion in total commitments
data.description: The EQT BPEA IX fund was oversubscribed and ultimately closed with total commitments reaching USD 15.6 billion. This fund now stands as the largest Asia Pacific-focused private equity fund ever raised, boasting USD 14.9 billion in fee-generating assets under management. The strong demand from investors showcases confidence in EQT's presence and performance in the Asia region.

data.title: CCE 2026 Wrapped Up with Record-Breaking Global Participation and Innovation
data.description: The 27th China Clean Expo (CCE 2026), held in Shanghai, wrapped up successfully on April 3, 2026. This event, organized by Sinoexpo Informa Markets and backed by ISSA – The Worldwide Cleaning Industry Association, took place at the Shanghai New International Expo Centre.

data.title: Omdia: India’s smartphone shipments fell 5% in 1Q26 amid channel caution and pricing pressures
data.description: In London, according to recent research by Omdia, smartphone shipments in India dropped by 5% year-on-year to 30.9 million units in the first quarter of 2026. This decline reflects a combination of weak seasonal demand and cautious inventory strategies among channels. The decrease in demand can be attributed to various macroeconomic challenges such as rupee depreciation and increasing inflation, which have impacted affordability and led to delayed consumer upgrades. Furthermore, preemptive stockpiling in anticipation of price hikes also constrained additional channel acquisitions. Notably, vivo maintained its market share amidst these challenging conditions.
